Core.description: The foundation of the portfolio. Broadly diversified holdings designed to provide long-term market exposure, stability, and compounding.

Income.description: Holdings focused on generating income through dividends or distributions while maintaining reasonable growth potential.

Quality.description: Established businesses with durable competitive advantages, strong balance sheets, and proven operating histories.
Quality.objective: Provide stability and reliable long-term growth.

Growth.description: Companies or funds with above-average growth expectations and higher potential upside, typically accompanied by higher volatility.

Alternatives.description: Assets that provide diversification away from traditional equities and income investments.
Alternatives.objective: Reduce concentration risk and improve diversification.

Cash.description: Uninvested capital available for future opportunities, liquidity needs, or portfolio rebalancing.
